title: Facilities Officer
description:
World Health SafetyTec (WHST) is a Singapore-based company providing EHS Consultation, Biological Indicator (BI) sterility testing, bizSAFE 3 certification support, and outsourcing of Safety & Medical Personnel. 

We partner with clients and businesses to strengthen workplace safety, regulatory compliance, and operational excellence.

For this position, selected candidate will be stationed onsite (Tuas) with a client partner as part of our outsourced services. This position works under the guidance of the Bio-containment Engineer and in close coordination with the Facilities & WHS Officer to ensure engineering systems run reliably, safely and in compliance with all regulatory requirements.

Key Responsibilities:

1. Warehouse and Inventory Management

i. Manage receiving, storage and issuance of equipment, engineering and facility-related materials, consumables, PPE, tools, and critical spares etc.

ii. Maintain accurate inventory records and stock levels using a digital inventory management system or manual logs.

iii. Conduct regular inventory audits and flag low stock or expired items.

iv. Coordinate with procurement staff and vendors to ensure timely replenishment of critical items.

2. Facilities & Engineering Support

i. Support daily facility inspections, troubleshooting and first-level fault diagnosis and reporting.

ii. Monitor and document key readings from engineering systems (e.g. temperature, pressure, flow rates, filter differential pressures).

3. Biosafety and WHS Coordination

i. Support the WHS Officer in implementing safety controls during maintenance or contractor works, including Permit-to-Work (PTW) and lock-out/tag-out procedures.

ii. Ensure work zones are kept in safe condition and assist with safety drills, inspections, and audits.

iii. Report near-misses or safety concerns and follow up on corrective actions.

4. Operational Readiness and Documentation

i. Assist in setup and testing of new equipment or infrastructure.

ii. Maintain facility-related logs and records, including maintenance

checklists, PTW forms, and service reports.

iii. Escort contractors and vendors during facility works and ensure compliance

with site safety rules.

5. General Facility Support

i. ii. Perform minor repairs, fixture replacements or workspace setups as needed. Respond to facility fault reports (e.g. power trip, HVAC issue, temperature alarm).

iii. Assist in general facility housekeeping, waste segregation logistics and pest control coordination etc.

Qualification & Experience Requirements:

1. Education & Experience:

• Nitec (National ITE Certificate), Higher Nitec, and Technical Diploma in Mechanical, Electrical, Facilities or Building Services Engineering.

• At least 2 years of relevant experience in technical facility operations.

• Experience in inventory or store management is highly preferred.

2. Key Skills & Attributes:

• Able to understand basic engineering skill, building services (M&E), basic electrical/mechanical troubleshooting.

• Able to use or learn basic inventory software/tools (e.g. Excel, barcoding, store systems).

• A strong sense of safety, organisation and attention to detail.

• Good communication and coordination skills.

Please include your current and expected salary in your resume. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Thank you for your understanding.
